page: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-24 is a graphics-optimized virtual machine featuring 24 vCPUs and 96 GB of system memory. The compute resource is powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor with 12 physical cores, operating at 2.2 GHz with hyper-threading enabled. This configuration includes a dedicated CPU allocation with 38 MB of L3 cache. Acceleration is delivered via two integrated NVIDIA L4 GPUs based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, providing a total of 45 GB of VRAM. Local storage is not bundled with this instance type, and it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data highlights a clear performance tradeoff between CPU-bound tasks and GPU-accelerated workloads. Single-core CPU performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by lower scores in stress-ng single-core, single-threaded gzip compression, and Geekbench single-core tests. However, the dual NVIDIA L4 GPUs deliver top-tier performance for large language model inference. Prompt processing speeds reach 11,848 tokens per second for small models and 248 tokens per second for large 70B models, while text generation remains strong across various model sizes. Multi-core CPU operations, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform in the average tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the g2-standard-24 offers a specialized hardware profile that maximizes GPU-to-compute density for AI workloads without requiring ultra-high-end enterprise GPU pricing. This makes it highly suitable for LLM inference, machine learning model deployment, ray tracing, and image processing. It is less optimal for single-threaded CPU tasks or workloads requiring high local NVMe storage.
description: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-24 is a graphics-optimized virtual machine configured with 24 vCPUs, 96 GB of RAM, and two NVIDIA L4 GPUs providing 45 GB of VRAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor with 12 physical cores running at 2.2 GHz, this instance does not include local storage. Performance benchmarks reveal a distinct tradeoff: single-core CPU performance is weak, but GPU-accelerated capabilities are exceptional. The instance achieves top-tier results in LLM inference, processing up to 11,848 tokens per second for small models and maintaining strong text generation speeds. Multi-core CPU tasks and memory bandwidth perform at average levels. Qualitatively, this server provides a cost-effective entry point for GPU-intensive applications by balancing moderate CPU capabilities with dual Ada Lovelace accelerators. It is best suited for LLM inference, machine learning deployment, ray tracing, and image processing workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-24 features 24 vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and two NVIDIA L4 GPUs. It delivers top-tier LLM inference performance despite weaker single-core CPU benchmarks.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-24 is a GPU-accelerated instance with 24 vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and dual NVIDIA L4 GPUs for LLM inference.
tagline: A dual-GPU accelerated virtual machine delivering top-tier LLM inference performance balanced with moderate multi-core x86_64 compute resources.
bullet_points: Equipped with two NVIDIA L4 GPUs providing 45 GB of total VRAM.
Features 24 vCPUs and 96 GB of RAM on an Intel Xeon x86_64 architecture.
Delivers top-tier LLM inference speeds for prompt processing and text generation.
Exhibits weaker single-core CPU performance, making it less ideal for single-threaded tasks.
No local storage is bundled with this instance configuration.
Optimized for machine learning deployment, ray tracing, and image processing.
